mnemonics.title: Data Type Sizes
mnemonics.content: INTEGRAL: INT 4, CHAR 1, SHORT 2, LONG 4/8. FLOAT 4, DOUBLE 8. REMEMBER: CHAR IS 1, INT IS 4, POINTER IS 8 ON 64-BIT.

mnemonics.title: Basic Data Types
mnemonics.content: IF YOU WANT TO STORE WHOLE NUMBERS, USE int. FOR DECIMALS, USE float OR double. FOR CHARACTERS, USE char. FOR NOTHING, USE void.

mnemonics.title: Type Modifiers
mnemonics.content: SHORT SHRINKS, LONG EXTENDS, SIGNED KEEPS SIGN, UNSIGNED LOSES NEGATIVES.

acronyms.expansion: American Standard Code for Information Interchange
acronyms.usage: Character encoding used by char data type, with values 0-127 representing standard characters.
Organization that standardized the C programming language (ANSI C).
Special value returned by file reading functions, typically -1, indicating no more data.

visualTips.visualization: Think of data types as containers of different sizes. Char is a small cup (1 byte), int is a medium bowl (4 bytes), double is a large bucket (8 bytes). Casts are like pouring liquid from one container to another - sometimes you lose precision (overflow) or gain accuracy.

Imagine computer memory as a row of numbered boxes. Each variable occupies a specific number of boxes based on its data type. A char takes 1 box, int takes 4 boxes, double takes 8 boxes. Each box can hold 1 byte of data.

Signed integers use one box for the sign (+/-) and the remaining boxes for the value. Unsigned uses all boxes for the value, doubling the maximum but losing negative numbers. Think of it like a car's odometer: unsigned counts up only, signed counts up and down.

analogies.analogy: Data types are like different containers in a kitchen. A char is like a small cup for single items, int is like a bowl for whole quantities, float/double is like measuring cups for fractional amounts. Using the wrong container wastes space or overflows.

Type casting is like converting currencies. Sometimes you lose precision (converting dollars to rupees, you might round the paise). Implicit casting is automatic (bank does it), explicit casting is manual (you calculate yourself).

analogies.anal analogy: The sizeof operator is like reading the label on a food package to see how many servings (bytes) it contains. It tells you the size without having to count every ingredient.
